rationale: Fail-direction lens: every detection leg fails CLOSED and no path fails open. Leg 1 checks the ScanOrphanedClosedBeads error FIRST (before orphans) so the mixed-list (orphans, firstErr) tuple still refuses; the core propagates epic-lookup/bd-list/ancestry errors while branchExistsFn stays the unchanged bool (absent-or-probe-fail = no trigger; deleted branch never false-refuses, AC2d). Leg 2 (worktree-enum) refuses on WorktreeList/ClosedEpicBeadIDs/ancestry error, refuses on any epic-scoped non-ancestor bead/ worktree, and NEVER consults branchExistsFn; epic-scoping (closed map keyed to this specID) means another spec's worktree neither triggers nor suppresses. Leg 3 refuses on planErr (naming the plan, NOT inheriting the :271 silent skip) AND on any CheckPendingObligations error — GetMetadata read error, present-null, corrupt, shape-invalid all fail-closed at source (uncoveredPendingObligations); recovery is branch-state-truthful via implBranchExistsFn (absent branch names restoration prerequisite, never a bare complete that dies at merge-base). Advisory (implAdvisorySlotLine) returns "" on every read failure, computes no decision, writes nothing, and its message contains neither MINDSPEC_SKIP_PANEL nor a paste-able refutation incantation. Hatches read nowhere in the gate. Placement before all mutations confirmed. Deviation C is a correct spec-mandated fail-closed tightening, not masking. Targeted fail-direction tests green.
findings: Leg 1 fail-closed CORRECT: err!=nil checked before len(orphans); ScanOrphanedClosedBeads returns (orphans, firstErr) on mixed lists and the gate refuses on the error regardless of orphan count — no fail-open. IsAncestor exit-1 vs other-error discrimination (gitops.go:446) preserved.

Leg 1 branchExistsFn stays fail-OPEN by design (round-6 C+B): absent OR probe-infra-failure = false = no trigger. Genuinely-deleted merged-and-cleaned branch never false-refuses. Correct per AC2d / R1 falsifier.

Leg 2 fail-closed CORRECT: WorktreeList err, ClosedEpicBeadIDs err, and per-branch ancErr each guard.NewFailure. Never reads branchExistsFn. Epic-scoping via closed[beadID] map (ClosedEpicBeadIDs(specID)) — a different spec's worktree is skipped (neither triggers nor suppresses), so epic-scoping cannot be suppressed by another spec.

Leg 3 fail-closed CORRECT: planErr!=nil refuses naming the plan (does NOT inherit ApproveImpl's :271 planErr==nil silent skip). Per-bead CheckPendingObligations fail-closed at source — read error, present-null pending/refuted, decode error, shape-invalid element all error (panel_advisory.go:522-634), never decode-as-empty. Recovery branch-state-truthful (implObligationRefusal: branch present -> complete; absent -> restoration prerequisite THEN complete). No bare command known to fail.

R2 advisory DECORATION-ONLY CONFIRMED: implAdvisorySlotLine returns "" on len(regs)==0, Tally err, or len(unresolved)==0 — a panel read error omits the slot line, never un-refuses, never crashes the decision (refusal error already constructed before the call). No metadata write, no gate decision. Message string carries no MINDSPEC_SKIP_PANEL and no paste-able refutation incantation (slot names only).

Hatch non-bypass CONFIRMED: runOrphanObligationGate / runWorktreeEnumerationLeg / the refusal helpers read neither MINDSPEC_SKIP_PANEL nor enforcement.panel_gate. Hatches bypass NOTHING in this gate; they retain 114 semantics only inside the recommended `mindspec complete` recovery run.

Placement CONFIRMED: gate at impl.go:369, after the last read-only gate (ADR-divergence :355) and before phase-reconcile write, epic close, phase=done, CommitCount, FinalizeEpic — a refusal performs no close/phase-write/merge/push. Only preceding write is phase.EnsureMigrated (:185), pre-existing and out of the refusal-guarantee scope.

Deviation A (plan.md fixture on ~13 tests): legitimate/ADDRESSED. Leg 3 reads plan.md fail-closed, so every ApproveImpl test now needs a plan.md (every real spec has one). Assertions unchanged — pure mechanical accommodation, no behavior hidden.

Deviation B (open->closed default bead status): legitimate/ADDRESSED. With a plan.md now present, gate-1/3's bead-status loop actually runs; happy-path tests must model a ready-for-impl-approve (all-closed) spec. Fixture correction, not masking.

Deviation C (NoCommitsNoBeads assertion change) — KEY: CORRECT spec-mandated tightening, NOT a masked regression. With no plan.md, planErr!=nil, so Leg 3 fail-closes at :369 BEFORE the CommitCount preflight at :422. R3's whole point is that an unreadable plan is never silently tolerated (falsifier: 'unreadable-plan-bead-IDs lets the gate silently skip'). New expectation (plan-bead-list-unreadable + recovery + FinalizeEpic uncalled) is exactly the fail-closed behavior. ADDRESSED.

Deviation D (OverrideADR added to DocSyncWarning test): legitimate/ADDRESSED from a fail-direction view. The now-present plan.md activates the ADR-divergence gate for the uncovered cmd/ fixture; override lets the test still pin the doc-sync WARN + FinalizeEpic-call rather than dying at the ADR gate. Does not alter the fail-direction under test.

Deviation E (7th seam implBranchExistsFn=gitutil.BranchExists): legitimate/ADDRESSED. Used ONLY by Leg 3's branch-state-truthful recovery message (implObligationRefusal), never as a detection trigger — consistent with R3 round-2 G3 and the implXxxFn convention. It does not weaken any fail-direction (recovery text only).

Empirical spot-check: go test ./internal/approve -run of OrphanRefuses|OrphanInfraErrorFailsClosed|WorktreeEnumRefusesDespiteProbeMiss|ObligationBackstop|CorruptPlanRefuses|HatchDoesNotBypassOrphan|AdvisorySlotNaming|DeletedBranchNoRefusal|PassesAfterOrphanSettled|CallOrder = PASS. Worktree clean (only untracked = reviews/).
